name: Maryland Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Maryland Board of Dietetic Practice
4201 Patterson Avenue
Baltimore, MD 21215-2299

renewalCycle: Every 2 years + 30 CPE hours (incl. implicit bias)

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From an accredited college or university, may be emailed to Lenelle.Cooper@maryland.gov or mailed to the Board.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR or CNS Registration Number
requiredDocuments.description: Your Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD/RDN) or Board for Certification of Nutrition Specialists (CNS) number for direct verification.

requiredDocuments.name: Completed Application Form
requiredDocuments.description: Paper applications require notarized signature page.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal History Records Check
requiredDocuments.description: May be required at the Board's discretion during the application process.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Maryland dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial combined application and licensure fee is $300 (a non-refundable one-time payment). Biennial renewal costs $250, which may include a $26 MHCC user fee.
The Board of Dietetic Practice, which operates under the Maryland Department of Health (MDH), is the regulatory body for dietitian licensure in Maryland.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Maryland dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licensees must complete a minimum of 30 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) during each two-year renewal cycle. This must include a one-time implicit bias CEU, which can count toward the 30-hour total. The Board may conduct random audits.

mainEntity.name: Does Maryland have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Maryland. To work in Maryland, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Maryland?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
